lede: A look at June 19 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Lou Gehrig and Blaise Pascal, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: WikiLeaks
events.desc: WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange requests asylum in London's Ecuadorian Embassy for fear of extradition to the US after publication of previously classified documents including footage of civilian killings by the US army; he will remain there until 2019.

events.title: Shishou incident
events.desc: Mass riots involving over 10,000 people and 10,000 police officers break out in Shishou, China, over the dubious circumstances surrounding the death of a local chef.

events.title: Pope John Paul II
events.desc: Pope John Paul II canonizes 117 Vietnamese Martyrs.

events.title: Nguyễn Cao Kỳ
events.desc: Nguyễn Cao Kỳ becomes Prime Minister of South Vietnam at the head of a military junta; General Nguyễn Văn Thiệu becomes the figurehead chief of state.

events.title: Kuwait
events.desc: Kuwait declares independence from the United Kingdom.

events.title: Cold War
events.desc: Cold War: Julius and Ethel Rosenberg are executed at Sing Sing, in New York.

events.title: Benito Mussolini
events.desc: Benito Mussolini, at the time a radical Socialist, is arrested by Bern police for advocating a violent general strike.

events.title: Abraham Lincoln
events.desc: President Abraham Lincoln signs the Territorial Slavery Act of 1862, which prohibits slavery in all current and future United States territories.

events.title: King's Chapel
events.desc: The Boston King's Chapel adopts James Freeman's revised prayer book, without the Nicene Creed, establishing it as the first Unitarian congregation in the United States.

events.title: 1718 Tongwei–Gansu earthquake
events.desc: At least 73,000 people died in the 1718 Tongwei–Gansu earthquake due to landslides in the Qing dynasty.

births.name: Christian Wulff
births.line: German lawyer and politician, 10th President of Germany

deaths.text: William Golding, British novelist, playwright, and poet, Nobel Prize laureate
Matthäus Merian the Elder, Swiss-German engraver and publisher
AD 404, Jin-dynasty warlord and emperor of Huan Chu

observances.text: Christian feast day: Deodatus (or Didier) of Nevers (or of Jointures)
Christian feast day: Gervasius and Protasius (Catholic Church)
